definitions.technical: The trigger point under proposed Section 15.12(c)(1) at which the extended redemption window activates. When redemption demands exceed 10% of outstanding issuance value within a 24-hour period, the issuer may take up to seven calendar days to process redemptions.
definitions.lay: A stress trigger built into the proposed rules. If more than 10% of all outstanding tokens are submitted for redemption within a single day, the stablecoin issuer gets extra time (up to seven days) to process the requests instead of the normal fast turnaround.
definitions.parenthetical: (the 24-hour redemption demand threshold that activates the seven-day extended processing window under proposed Section 15.12(c)(1))
definitions.legal_significance: The 10% threshold establishes a quantitative definition of a stablecoin stress event for regulatory purposes. The comment letter notes that while this provides a useful circuit breaker, the seven-day extension may still be inadequate if reserves are frozen in FDIC receivership proceedings.
